## Cases  of cryptosporidiosis in Canada
Since 2003, reports of cryptosporidiosis in Canada averaged almost 2 cases per  100,000 people annually.

## How we monitor cryptosporidiosis in Canada
Canada  uses different  surveillance systems to monitor cases of food-borne illness. These include the:
- National  Enteric Surveillance Program (NESP)
- Canadian  Notifiable Disease Surveillance System
Cryptosporidiosis is a nationally notifiable disease and  is reported by all provinces and territories.
Cases are reported to:
- provincial or territorial departments of health
- the federal government, if they meet the national case definition
